If getting the antidote through dialogue with Nettie, she now offers to let the party go free as long as they swear to commit suicide prior to turning. Refusing once again leads to a fight.
Brewing an antidote
It is possible to learn to brew an antidote. In the room where the party originally found Nettie healing the bird, there is a book named  On Antidotes on a desk by the cauldron. Reading the book teaches how to craft an  Antidote. Combining  Salts of Mugwort with any suspension allows to brew an antidote. Antidotes found elsewhere also work for curing the poison.
Three  Mugworts can be found in the Druid's Quarters to extract the salts and the room Nettie locks the party in has three  Bullywug Trumpets that allow extracting a suspension, providing all the ingredients needed to brew an antidote. Picking up the Bullywug Trumpet nearest to Nettie triggers a dialogue line where she tells the party to stop searching for an antidote as they will not find one.
It is possible to brew the antidote ahead of speaking with Nettie or simply bring one found elsewhere in the world. Should the player character get poisoned, they then have the option to cure themselves immediately, which causes Nettie to give up, and loop back to offering the party a chance to leave if they swear to kill themselves should they start to transform.
The party is still trapped within the library. To escape, they must loot Nettie's Circlet, the  Key of the Ancients which opens the stone slab doors. The party can use this to return to the Druid Grove, or head towards the Underground Passage.
Key of the Ancients
Wyvern Toxin
The Cauldron of Boiling Theriac used to brew the Elixir of Silvanus in early versions.
Prior to Patch #1 the party was able to read the book  Flora of the Dalelands in the Druid's Quarters to learn that the poison Nettie has used on them is known as 'Kelemvor's Kiss'. The book also taught the party how to brew an  Elixir of Silvanus by adding a  Mugwort Bundle to the Cauldron of Boiling Theriac to cure the poison. This would also have granted the inspiration "The Apprentice Turns Master" for the Guild Artisan background.

The Rite of Thorns is dangerous magic. Why is Kagha so determined to do this? We should investigate.
We found a note addressed to Kagha that mentioned a meeting in the swamp. What is she up to?
We found a note proving that Kagha is in league with the Shadow Druids. This could explain why she wants the tieflings gone.
A second note proved that Kagha is in league with the Shadow Druids. This could explain why she wants the tieflings gone.
(Kagha lives) We persuaded Kagha to turn against the Shadow Druids. It's time to end this.
(Kagha hostile) Kagha refused to abandon her plans. It's time to end this.
We defeated the Shadow Druids and stopped the ritual. We should report our success to Zevlor.
Quest Complete: (Kagha lived) We exposed Kagha but were able to convince her to abandon the Shadow Druids. After fighting them, she stopped the ritual. The grove remains open.
(Kagha died) We exposed Kagha and fought the Shadow Druids. Kagha is dead, and her ritual has been stopped. The grove remains open.
The tieflings have been forced out of the grove, and the druids' ritual is complete. The grove is closed now - perhaps for good.
We've interrupted the druids' ritual to close the grove, but now they are attacking the tieflings!
The grove is besieged by goblins! The druids will want nothing more to do with us.
We defeated the goblins. The refugees can safely leave the grove.

Investigate Kagha.Walkthrough:
This multi-staged quest can have several different outcomes, though it roughly follows the same path. Upon receiving this quest, the party is implored to search the Emerald Grove to investigate Kagha.
The vital clue required to progress this quest lies in the  Half-Torn Note in Kagha's chest at X:-484Y:-7 in the Emerald Grove. Interacting with the chest is considered a crime, meaning the party must engage with it while out of sight, if they wish to avoid attitude lost. This can be done by hiding and entering turn-based mode, as the chest is tucked away in a dark corner behind a bookcase. The chest is locked and requires a  DC10 Sleight of Hand Check to open. If the party cannot pass the Skill Check, they can alternatively destroy the chest.
Reading the note reveals that Kagha is corresponding with someone named  Olodan, who asked her to meet alone at the "swamp". The "swamp" is located in the Putrid Bog at X:97Y:224, in the southern part of the Overworld map.
The swamp is guarded by 2 wood woads and 4 ancient mud mephits who are hostile on sight. The wood woads can regenerate 10 Hit Points per turn, but are vulnerable to fire damage which breaks their regeneration. The ancient mud mephits are fragile, but will endlessly summon young mud mephits until they are killed. Furthermore, mud and tangling weeds predominate the battlefield, creating Difficult Terrain. On higher difficulty levels, achieving Surprise with invisibility or other means can buy enough time to whittle away the enemies before they respond.
Once the enemies are defeated, a successful  DC5 Perception Check at the large tree in the center of the island at X:86Y:220 reveals a wooden crevice that hides a  Letter to Kagha. The letter reveals that Kagha has been scheming with the Shadow Druids to take over the Emerald Grove and install herself as First Druid.
With Kagha's secret revealed, all that is required to finish this quest is to confront her. Confronting Kagha forces the Shadow Druids  Olodan,  Wren, and  Pinta — who were  Wild Shaped as rats — to reveal themselves. The party can try to persuade Kagha to repent and break her alliance with the Shadow Druids before the fight, turning her into an ally in the upcoming confrontation.
To do so, they must pass one of the following checks to stop her from immediately entering combat:
[OATH OF THE ANCIENTS][PERSUASION]Harmony cannot 'defile'. It is nature's will, and you would pervert it, Kagha.(DC 15)(non-Oathbreaker only)
[PALADIN][PERSUASION]You are druids. You've an oath to uphold nature's harmony, its strength - stand by it.(DC 15)(non-Oathbreaker only)
[DRUID][PERSUASION]Harmony is not weakness - it is balance. Strength. Silvanus calls on you to preserve it.(DC 10)
[SHADOWHEART][PERSUASION]You are not strong enough to embrace the shadows. You will only become corrupted in the eyes of your peers. Cease.(DC 15)
[PERSUASION]The shadows won't save you - they'll corrupt you. Stop this, while you can.(DC 15)
The party must also succeed on another check to convince her to join their side, unless they picked one of the following options which bypass the second check:
[DRUID][NATURE]Can't you feel how the prayer disturbs the soil and air? The grove trembles in fear.(DC 15)  (bypasses second check)
[DETECT THOUGHTS][INTELLIGENCE]Probe Kagha's mind.
You're so desperate to be proven right - it's made you forget who you really are.
Convincing Kagha to switch sides
If the first check is passed, the party member makes a passive  DC10 Insight Check check to see if Kagha sees reason in their words. The party can then attempt to convince her to switch sides.
[WOOD HALF-ELF][WOOD ELF][DRUID][BARD]Recite the frescos you have read. Remind Kagha of the grove's legacy.(if frescos were read/understood)
[HISTORY]Recite the frescos you have read. Instil her with pride in the grove's legacy.(DC 5)(if frescos were read/understood)
[RELIGION]I know these druids' ways. All they touch is fated to decay.(DC 10)(if Insight check passed)
[RELIGION]I know these druids' ways. In their hands, this grove will decay.(DC 10)(if Insight check not passed)
[PERSUASION]You know I'm right. Don't let the shadows corrupt you.(DC 10)(if Insight check passed)
[SHADOWHEART][PERSUASION]You are not strong enough to embrace the shadows. You will only become corrupted in the eyes of your peers. Cease.(DC 15)
[PERSUASION]The shadows will only corrupt - you don't need them.(DC 15)(if Insight check not passed)
If the party read the frescos in the Emerald Grove and learned of the Grove's history, passing passive checks on at least two of them, the top two dialogue options become unlocked, allowing an easier check.
Successfully convincing Kagha to switch sides grants the following inspiration:
A Story for the Ages ( Entertainer backgrounds)
Entitled ( Noble backgrounds)
Combat
After that, a fight breaks out against the three Shadow Druids, with Kagha either joining them or the party depending on the dialogue choices. The other occupants of the Emerald Grove, such as  Rath and  Silver, also join the party against the Shadow Druids.
After the battle, if Kagha survives and the player character is a druid, she bestows upon them the title of Faithwarden and awards them with the staff,  Pale Oak. If Kagha perished during the battle, the party can loot the magical amulet  Broodmother's Revenge off her body.
Broodmother's Revenge, looted from Kagha's body
Hellrider's Pride, awarded by Zevlor
Pale Oak, awarded to the player if they are a druid
15 Exp for confronting Kagha
Kill Kagha
If Kagha is convinced to stand down through reference to the frescos and nature's teachings -1 +1 +5 +1
If Kagha is confronted and attacked +1 +1 +1
The letter to Kagha in the Putrid Bog is there even if the party have not started this quest. With meta knowledge or thorough exploration of the Sunlit Wetlands, it is possible to "skip ahead" by finding it unprompted. The quest begins and the party can confront Kagha.
The fight with the Shadow Druids can be avoided but may cause a bug. If shooting one of the rats just before the final conversation with Kagha, the rats run away. The conversation with the Shadow Druids still occurs, but when it is finished and if Kagha is convinced to aid the party in the fight, there is no fight. However, the quest remains in the journal and the party cannot obtain the Pale Oak if they were otherwise eligible for it.
If the goblin leaders are killed or knocked out before the party confront Kagha, the quest resolves automatically without the party needing to return to Halsin as the quest log describes. Zevlor does not reward the party. The Shadow Druids leave and no further interaction besides Kagha is available.

"We heard strange music on the beach. Where is it coming from?"
"We discovered a tiefling child in a strange trance."
"We rescued the child from the harpies. He is alive and well."
"The child said we should pay a visit to Mol. We should tell a child named Doni that we want to see the 'Dragon's Lair'."
"Doni revealed a hatch and opened it for us - the 'Dragon's Lair' must be below."
"Doni won't be able to show us how to get into the 'Dragon's Lair'. We'll need to find a way to Mol ourselves."
"Doni disappeared somewhere. We'll need to find another way to get to Mol - or maybe Doni will be back eventually?"
"We know where Mol is hiding. Time to pay her a visit."
Quest Complete: "We found Mol. She was more than happy to meet us since we rescued one of her people."
"Safe from the harpies, the child left the area."
"The tiefling child died."
"We weren't able to investigate the beach."
"We heard strange music on the beach, but didn't investigate further."
"We left the region without discovering what happened to the child."
"We won't be having any conversations with Mol any time soon."
"The situation has changed in the grove. Mol is busy dealing with other matters."

Investigate the Beach.Walkthrough:
The Secluded Cove is north-east of the druidic ritual circle in the Emerald Grove. Upon approaching, strange singing can be heard at X:271Y:523. Near the water, a child  Mirkon is enchanted by the singing of Harpies at X:327Y:549. The party can try to focus on the singing or tell Mirkon to return to shore.
I don't hear anything. Come back to shore.
Focus and try to hear what he hears.
[ROGUE][SLEIGHT OF HAND]Pick his pockets while he's distracted.(DC 5)
If the party focus on the singing, they can try to resist the song:
[WISDOM]Struggle against the music's pull.(DC 5)
[WISDOM]Struggle against the music's pull.(DC 5)(if passive DC 10 Nature roll passed)
[DRACONIC BLOODLINE][CHARISMA]Foul creatures - mere carrion birds compared to your bloodline. Focus the will of the dragon against their song.(DC 5)
[SORCERER][CHARISMA]Focus your will. Improvise a magical shell to dampen the music.(DC 5)
[BARD][PERFORMANCE]Focus by singing: 'There once was a lady who met with a merrow, and tried to disclose it to her foolish fellow...'(DC 5)
If the party successfully resist the song, they can attempt to overpower or distract Mirkon:
[BARD][PERFORMANCE]Focus by singing: 'There once was a lady who met with a merrow, and tried to disclose it to her foolish fellow...'(DC 5)
[BARD][PERFORMANCE] '... Her man would not listen, he thought it tall tales, until he rubbed up against all of her scales!' (DC 10)
[BERSERKER][PERFORMANCE]ROAR. OVER. THE VOICE.(DC 10)
[BARBARIAN][PERFORMANCE]Drown out the harpies with a ROAR.(DC 10)
Regardless of what he is told, Mirkon attempts to move closer to the harpies.
The harpies utilize a combination of the terrain and their  Luring Song to bring Mirkon and the party into close range. One harpy always remains at a distance to perform the song, causing party members to waste their turns if they fail to resist. Meanwhile, the other harpies fly close to attack the party. Most attempts to approach distant harpies are slowed because of the area's Difficult Terrain. However, pre-combat castings of  Enhanced Leap (in conjuntion with high Strength and  Feather Fall) as well as strategic high-ground positioning of melee characters may alleviate this issue. The harpies can  Fly over the distance and terrain, which allows them to easily  Multiattack.
Should melee combat be delayed or unavailable, ranged attacks and spells cast on the singing harpy can also be used to break its Concentration. If broken, another harpy likely sings, if not currently engaged in melee.
Additional preparation for combat may involve  blessing the party before speaking with Mirkon to improve resistance to Luring Song. Using gnomes, elves, or half-elven characters with Advantage on saves can improve the likelihood of resisting the song.
An aggressive yet viable strategy to protect Mirkon involves throwing or shoving him away from the shoreline and the harpies. Throwing him provides more distance from the enemies and may reduce his likelihood of being killed, even if damaged by the fall, which causes him to go prone for several turns.
Casting  Calm Emotions protects Mirkon and all party members within its radius from being affected by Luring Song, significantly reducing combat difficulty (the game log still shows a saving throw being rolled, but the characters are unaffected on failures).
Alternatively, the party can use  Silence and remain within the silenced area. In both cases, the party should be careful to prevent the caster's Concentration from being broken. If the party member casting Calm Emotions or Silence is a cleric, they may first cast  Sanctuary on themselves to prevent broken Concentration.
Casting Silence over Mirkon before speaking with him immediately triggers the fight, skipping all dialogue. If deciding on this strategy, it is advised to cast Silence not only over Mirkon but on the entire party, too.
Once the harpies are defeated, Mirkon informs the party that he needs to get back because  Mol will be mad at him. Before leaving, he tells the party to find  Doni and say him that they want to see the "Dragon's Lair".
If the party trigger this quest and then travel back to camp,[verify] Mirkon dies. He also dies if Save the Refugees is resolved by completing the Rite of Thorns or by evading the goblin attack.
Access to the tiefling children's hideout, the "Dragon's Lair"
Protector of the Small ( Folk Hero background)
Slaying the Piper ( Urchin background)
This quest is triggered when crossing at X:299Y:542; taking any long rest after it is triggered results in Mirkon's death.
Mirkon's corpse can be found near the harpies' spawn during Find Doni, if he is not saved prior.
Helping Mirkon is an easy way to gain favor with Mol and initiate Steal the Sacred Idol.
If Mirkon is killed by a third party during the battle with the harpies, the quest is bugged, both ending in success and teleporting a now-revived Mirkon away from the beach. This is easy to trigger by summoning Lump and his ogre band during the battle; they attack a Lured Mirkon, killing him, triggering the bug in the process.
Although he has no notable possessions, pickpocketing Mirkon while he is entranced grants The Allure of Coin for party members with the  Criminal background.
The bard's ability to use Performance against the harpies is a reference to the bard's ability in past editions called "Counter Song".
While talking to Mirkon, using Improvised Melee Weapon with another character and moving him away from the beach actually breaks him from the harpies' trance and completes the quest without having to fight them. The harpies are then revealed in the map afterwards.

Kill Kagha: The tieflings and druids are at an impasse. Killing Kagha might tip the balance.
We exposed Kagha as a traitor. Furious, she attacked us.
We attacked Kagha, and the druids turned on all of the tieflings.
Kagha and the other druids are dead. The conflict in the grove has ended. We should check on the tieflings.
We killed Kagha and her Shadow Druid associates. Zevlor will be glad to hear it.
Quest Complete: The goblins are marching on the grove. Kagha and Zevlor are too focused on their own people to worry about each other.
Zevlor thanked us for taking care of Kagha. Now we can focus on the goblin threat.
With Zevlor gone, there will be no reward for killing Kagha.
We persuaded Kagha to stop the ritual. The conflict has ended - the tieflings can stay in the grove.
With the road cleared, the tieflings can leave in peace.
Too many of the tieflings died. Unable to stay, the rest were forced from the grove.

Kill Kagha.Walkthrough:
The quest to kill  Kagha can be received after speaking with  Zevlor once Kagha requests the party assist the tieflings in moving on. The party can ask Zevlor "Can you delay the ritual?" and he explains that it is Kagha's influence that is causing the intolerance to outsiders. Selecting "Then why don't you get rid of her?" has Zevlor agreeing, but stating that the tieflings cannot enter the Inner Sanctum, while the party can. The party can either take him up on the offer or decline.
After this conversation, Kagha can be killed at any time. However, if Kagha is attacked unprovoked, the other druids come to her aid and a fight is started between the tieflings and the druids. The fight happens in real time so the party members need to move quickly after killing Kagha at the Inner Sanctum if they want to save as many tieflings as possible. In all cases  Rath sides with the tieflings during the confrontation, as well as  Findal if he is found and healed in the Underground Passage.
If the party complete Investigate Kagha and find evidence of her collaborating with the Shadow Druids, then Kagha can be confronted without the druids becoming hostile. The party has a chance to sway Kagha to their side, otherwise the party can simply state that she is too far gone and fight her to the death with the other Shadow Druids. If she is slain,  Loic and  Marcoryl join her. Either choice results in the Rite of Thorns being stopped, and the tieflings are allowed to safely leave the Grove when they are ready.
Broodmother's Revenge (Looted from Kagha's body)
The inspiration Serpent's Bounty for party members with the  Criminal background once the reward for killing Kagha is accepted from Zevlor.
Investigate Kagha

"The adventurer gave in and revealed the location of the grove. We have what the drow Minthara needs to order the raid."
"Minthara asked us to interrogate the prisoner. She wants to know where the adventurers were hiding out."
"Minthara is preparing to attack the grove and told us to wait for next light. We should get some rest."
"Sazza told the drow Minthara where the grove is. Minthara plans to raid it - and wants our help."
"Minthara and her warband have set out for the Grove. They are waiting for us to infiltrate it."
"We arrived at the grove just in time. Minthara's raiders will be here soon."
"The tieflings are as ready as they can be. All that's left is to sound the horn."
"Minthara arrived with raiders by her side. Defeating them means safety at last for the tieflings."
"The goblins were beaten back, and the tieflings lived to tell the tale. Zevlor/Asharak/Cerys wants to talk."
"We chose Minthara and the Absolute over the tieflings. Now to take the grove."
"The tieflings are dead, but the druids are making a last stand at their sacred pool."
"Tieflings and druids alike lie dead. The grove belongs to the Absolute. Minthara will be pleased."
"The grove was overrun before we could get involved. We should speak to Minthara."
"Faced with a barricade of vines, the goblins couldn't raid the grove. Minthara isn't happy."
Quest Complete: "The goblin threat has ended, and the tieflings are ready to leave. Tonight, we celebrate our victory at camp."
"With the tieflings dead, the grove is ours. Pleased, the Absolute granted us new powers. Tonight we celebrate at camp."
"Minthara was furious that the grove was sealed and attacked us."
"Minthara died before she could lead an attack on the grove."
"Minthara died before she could reward us for our part in the attack on the grove."

Raid the Emerald Grove.Walkthrough:
Raiding the Emerald Grove can be started either by talking to  Minthara in the Shattered Sanctum alone, or by completing Save the Goblin Sazza and accompanying  Sazza to inform Minthara about the Grove. Once the goblin forces are aware of the Grove's location, they mobilize to raid it. Minthara bids the party go back to the Grove and infiltrate it from within. The raid occurs after the next Long Rest.
Once back at the Grove, the Druids and Tieflings have moved in response to the goblin's movements. The druids lock themselves into the Inner Sanctum and refuse to shelter any outlanders.  Rikka is gathering the tiefling children, starting Find Doni if the party agree to help her.
To start the raid, the party must speak to  Zevlor on the ramparts at X:192Y:446 It is possible to tell him the party betrayed the tieflings, starting the battle early, or speak with him further for more information on his strategy. If Zevlor believes the party members are on his side, he instructs them to sound the war horn once they are ready to start the raid. Immediately after sounding the horn the goblins appear, as well as Minthara, who orders the party to open the gate.
The party can obey:
Obey and open the gate.
[EVIL CLERIC][EVIL PALADIN]Of course - as does my god.
[OATHBREAKER]They still think I'm on their side. Poor fools.
The party can also refuse instead:
No. This grove is under my protection.
[SHADOWHEART]I serve Shar, not the Absolute. The gate stays closed.
[CLERIC]Your god has no power here.
[ASTARION]New plan - I tear out your throat.
[LAE'ZEL]There's been a change of plan.
[MINSC]I will not. Boo will not. And if you think to triumph here today, drow - you will not. (impossible without mods)
[WYLL]Not so fast. You've just walked into my trap.
[KARLACH]These people are under my protection - but I'll see you in the Hells.
[GOOD CLERIC][NEUTRAL CLERIC][GOOD PALADIN][NEUTRAL PALADIN]No. I'll show the Absolute the power of a true god.
[DRUID]This place is sacred to Silvanus - the Absolute has no power here.
[PALADIN]I will not bring death to this sacred place, Minthara.
If the party open the gate, Minthara and the Goblin raiders become allies while the tieflings and Wyll (if present) become enemies. The party must, together with Minthara and the goblins, move through the grove in sections clearing out all that remain. On returning to Minthara after the battle she tells the party she will meet them at camp for the victory celebration with the goblins. There Minthara congratulates the party and rewards them with a gift from the Absolute - a True Soul tadpole. After the night ends, Minthara tells the party that they must go to Moonrise Towers to find their place in the Absolute's Grand Design. She provides a  Spider's Lyre and explains that a servant of hers can lead the party through the Shadow-Cursed Lands where Moonrise Towers is located. This instrument can also be pickpocketed from her, which results in a second instrument being generated should she later be recruited as a companion. Then, she leaves, stating that she is gathering the troops to meet the party at Moonrise Towers. This starts Travel to Moonrise Towers.
Refusing her instructions and protecting the grove results in the tieflings acting as allies. After the battle the surviving tieflings speak to the party and thank them for their help, then celebrate at camp. If  Halsin is alive, he appears to congratulate the party and explains the strange movements occurring at Moonrise Towers. He tells the party the best shot at curing their tadpoles is going there, and offers to go with them.

Gift from the Absolute - if the goblins are sided with
 Minthara can become a Companion in Act Two, if the goblins are sided with or if choosing not to get involved in the raid either way. Minthara can also be recruited if she is not killed in the fight but knocked out with non-lethal melee attacks.
Agreeing to help raid the Emerald Grove  +1 -5 -1 -1
Choosing to raid the grove with the goblin forces has immense, far-reaching consequences for the game, some of which can only be felt in later Acts. These consequences include:
Karlach and  Wyll leave the party (or cannot be recruited if they have not already been).
 Halsin is not available as a companion.
 Gale leaves the party unless an Insight or Deception check is passed.
Lift the Shadow Curse cannot be resolved in Act Two.
Loss of many rare equipment items, as the traders  Dammon and  Mattis cannot be traded with in Acts Two and Three.
Halsin,  Arabella, Dammon, and  Zevlor cannot be recruited for Gather Your Allies.
 Rolan cannot be recruited for Gather Your Allies if he was convinced to stay and defend the Grove earlier.
All unfinished quests involving the Grove cannot be successfully completed.
The inspiration Slaughter's Prize is granted to party members with the Criminal background.
The inspiration Camaraderie in Bloodthirst is granted to the  Dark Urge.
Choosing to bring the goblins to the Emerald Grove, but then siding with the tieflings and druids lets the story play out similarly to as if the party had defeated the goblin camp leaders, but without the need to defeat the other two leaders  Gut and  Dror Ragzlin. However, some of the tieflings might die in the fight.
Siding with the Emerald Grove also results in  Minthara being available as a companion in Act Two, if she is not killed but  Knocked Out instead.
Many but not all goblins who accompany Minthara on the raid can be encountered only during the raid.
If the party return, the entire Goblin Camp is hostile to the party.
A somewhat different set of consequences is incurred if choosing to leave the region without participating in the fight at the grove, including:
All tieflings of the Emerald Grove die.
Karlach and Wyll stay in the party (or can still be recruited if Act Two has not been started yet by travelling to the Shadow-Cursed Lands).
Karlach's Infernal Engine cannot be repaired because Dammon died.
Halsin cannot be recruited as companion, and spontaneously dies in his last known location, even if previously saved from the Goblins.
Gale permanently leaves the party.
The quest Lift the Shadow Curse cannot be successfully completed.
Loss of many rare equipment items, as the then dead traders Dammon and Mattis will not be present in Acts Two or Three.
Halsin, Arabella, Dammon, and Zevlor cannot be recruited for Gather Your Allies.
Rolan cannot be recruited for Gather Your Allies if he was convinced to stay and defend the Grove earlier.
All unfinished quests involving the Emerald Grove cannot be successfully completed.
Despite refusing Minthara's orders, she is still available as a potential companion in Act Two.
A specific bit of dialogue happens with the being in the  Astral Prism, but all other characters appear to ignore the decision.

"We met an adventurer called Barth who accused a tiefling child of stealing his locket. We suspected the child but couldn't get the locket back."
"An adventurer called Barth accused a tiefling child of stealing his locket. The adventurer was knocked out by a guard, and the child got away with the locket."
"An adventurer called Barth accused a tiefling child of stealing his locket. Surprisingly, we found the locket on the child - Barth will want it back."
"We found a locket stashed away - it's probably the same one Barth had stolen from him."
"Unsurprisingly, we found the stolen locket in the child's pockets. Barth will want it back."
Quest Complete: "We returned the missing locket to Barth - free of charge."
"Barth begrudgingly rewarded us for finding his locket."
"We ignored Barth's protests and kept the locket."
"Barth was killed before we could return his locket."
"We never discovered what happened to the adventurer's locket - or whether the child was lying."
"We weren't able to return the locket to the adventurer."

Return the Locket.Walkthrough:
When the party first enter the Hollow, an event occurs with one of  Aradin's adventurers,  Barth, and a young tiefling child,  Meli. Upon approaching Barth at X:168Y:544, Meli pickpockets him, stealing a  Brass Locket. Barth realizes that he has been robbed and chases the child. If nothing is done, Barth eventually attacks Meli and is dragged away by guards.
The party can intervene by speaking with Barth or Meli. Meli continuously denies that he has the locket, while Barth insists it was him. The dialogue options change depending on whether or not Arabella is alive.
If Meli is sided with, Barth is angered and Meli escapes:
Don't you dare lay a finger on that child
[PERSUASION]You're the one making threats - to a child. Pull yourself together.(DC 10) +1 +1 +1
[PALADIN][INTIMIDATION]Whatever you do to the child, I'll repay ten-fold.(DC 5) +1 +1 +1
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]Strike the child, go on. See what happens.(DC 10) +1 +1 +1
[INTIMIDATION]Raise a hand to that child and you'll lose it. Your choice.(DC 10) +1 +1 +1
[TIEFLING][PERSUASION]Just warning. I've dealt with your type before, human.(DC 10) +1 +1 +1
If the party decide to take Barth's side and pass a Persuasion ability check, they can ask Meli why he took the locket. Barth is initially dismissive, but Meli claims that it looked like his mom's, and Barth decides to let Meli keep the locket. After this scene Meli laughs, because his bluff worked.
You better do what he says, boy.
[PERSUASION]You're not fooling anyone, boy. Why'd you take it?(DC 5)
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]That bauble worth a thrashing?(DC 15)
[INTIMIDATION]Do you really want to take a beating over some jewellery?(DC 15)
[TIEFLING][PERSUASION]Just give it back, boy. You're better than this.(DC 5)
[BERSERKER][INTIMIDATION]DO AS HE SAYS, OR JOIN THE SNAKEBITTEN GIRL IN HER GRAVE.(DC 5)
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]Thieving children wind up dead around here. Do as he says.(DC 5)
[INTIMIDATION]Better do what he says, boy. There's already one thief to bury today.(DC 5) -1 -1 -1
[TIEFLING][PERSUASION]Just give it back, boy. You're better than this.(DC 5)
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]That bauble worth a thrashing?(DC 15)
[INTIMIDATION]Do you really want to take a beating over some jewellery?(DC 15)
[PERSUASION]You're not fooling anyone, boy. Why'd you take it?(DC 5)
Monks can ask for everyone to calm down, and then composedly ask for cooler heads:
[MONK]I think it would benefit everyone to calm down here. What happened?(if Arabella is alive)
[MONK]Theft and revenge have already killed a child here. Calm yourselves - explain what happened.(if Arabella died)
There are similar options as if Barth was sided with, however there is another option:
[MONK][PERSUASION]Let us find balance. The child will return the locket - and the adult will act his age.(DC 15)
Alternatively, the party can opt to do nothing, which leads to an altercation where Barth slaps Meli, who then calls for  Ikaron.
I tried to give you a chance. You're on your own. -1 -1
Keep quiet and watch. -1 -1
Forget it. I don't want any trouble.
If failing to intimidate Meli or not getting involved,  Ikaron approaches. He asks what is going on; the party can either call Meli a thief, and convince the guard to side with Barth by passing a  DC15 Persuasion or Intimidation Check, or side with Meli to let him get off free.
Additionally, the party can pickpocket Meli before speaking to him, in which case a new dialogue option appears:
Relax. The boy's good, but I'm better - here's your locket.
If this happens, Meli is suitably impressed and runs off; the locket can be returned to Barth.
Lastly, if Meli escapes, the locket is stored inside a thief's chest at the Tiefling Hideout. Lockpicking the chest and stealing the locket back allows the party to return the locket to Barth. He then is grateful and also thanks party if they stopped him from slapping Meli because he lost his cool.
You man this locket? How much is it worth to you?
Thank you kindly. +1 -1
Forget the coin. The locket's yours. Take it. (no reward)
[DETECT THOUGHTS]Read his mind.(DC 10)
I think you can up your offer a little. It was a gift from your mother, after all. -1 +1
Here's the locket. It's all yours.
Heirloom, is it? Been in your family long?
[PERSUASION]Not much of an heirloom then... unless there's a tale behind it, maybe?(DC 15)
[DETECT THOUGHTS]Read his mind.(DC 10)
[PERSUASION]Come on, spit it out. What aren't you telling me about the locket?(DC 10)
Stealing the locket back and giving it back to Barth while he is talking to Meli:  0 gp
Bringing the locket back after stealing it from the Tiefling Hideout:
If asking for nothing Barth gives the party  25 gp.
If asking for reward, Barth pays  50 gp.
Blood Price  ( Criminal backgrounds)  - if telling Barth that the party got the locket back, but not giving / selling it back to him he becomes hostile.
Family Comes First ( Urchin backgrounds) - if Meli was convinced to tell the truth about the amulet.
Protect Your Own ( Urchin backgrounds) - for protecting Meli

"We learned that a child named Arabella is being held by the druids on charges of theft. We should check on her."
"We found Arabella in the druid's lair. She's being judged by Kagha, the leader of  the druids, and is clearly in trouble."
Intevene: "We managed to save Arabella, who immediately ran to her parents."
"Arabella is safe - her parents are grateful for our intervention."

Do nothing: "Kagha's snake killed Arabella. Her parents should be informed."
Tell the truth: "We told Arabella's parents what happened. They were devastated."
Tell a lie: "We lied and told Arabella's parents that she was fine. They'll learn the bad news eventually - but not from us."

Save Arabella.Walkthrough:
Upon approaching the Druid's Chambers, the party can encounter a group of Tiefling Refugees arguing with the druids inside.  Komira and  Locke ask the party to head into the Chambers and check on their daughter  Arabella. Arabella was caught stealing the  Idol of Silvanus, a component that can be used in a magical ritual; the druids are using it in this way to seal off the Grove from outsiders.
The druids at the gate initially try to stop the party but, unless attacked or ignored repeatedly, soon relent and let them through. Upon entering the Druid's Chambers, Arabella can be found standing in judgement in front of  Kagha. Also present are some other druids and Kagha's restless, venomous snake  Teela. The party can watch and do nothing, in which case Teela bites and kills Arabella. If choosing to attack, a large-scale fight begins between the druids and the tieflings in the whole grove, where the druid  Rath then fights on the side of the tieflings.
To avoid both, the death of Arabella and the start of a fight, the party can attempt to reason with Kagha:
[LAE'ZEL][INTIMIDATION]Free the whelp, or I run your belly through with a blade.(DC 10)
[DRUID][NATURE]The girl is no threat. Jailing her disrupts nature's balance.(DC 10)
[NATURE]Don't druids cherish harmony? Jailing the girl disrupts nature's balance.(DC 12)
[TIEFLING][NATURE]Don't druids cherish harmony? Jailing the girl disrupts nature's balance.(DC 15)
[PERSUASION]Release her. I'll see that she stays out of trouble.(DC 12)
[DRUID][PERSUASION]Release her. I'll see that she stays out of trouble.(DC 10)
[TIEFLING][PERSUASION]Release her. I'll see that she stays out of trouble.(DC 18)
[DRUID][PERSUASION]A seed nobly sown renders the sweetest fruit. Be noble - set her free.(DC 10)
[DRUID][TIEFLING][PERSUASION]A seed nobly sown renders the sweetest fruit. Be noble - set her free.(DC 15)
[OATH OF THE CROWN][PERSUASION]I am sworn to uphold the law. I will judge this transgression.(DC 10)
[PALADIN][PERSUASION]I am versed in the ways of justice. Make your case, and I will pass judgement.(DC 10)
[MONK][PERSUASION]You act rashly - as a cornered viper would. Free the child or more tieflings will interrupt you.(DC 10)
[BARD][PERSUASION]Silvanus was more concerned with 'nobly sown seeds' than punishing children, was He not?(DC 15)
[BERSERKER][INTIMIDATION]RELEASE HER. NOW.(DC 15)
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]She's just a cub. Let her go, or tangle with me.(DC 15)
[DETECT THOUGHTS][WISDOM]Read Kagha's mind.
You've proven your authority. Now prove your mercy.
[DRUID][ANIMAL SPEAKING]Tell the snake to put Kagha at ease. The girl belongs with her own brood.
If the check is failed, there is another chance to convince Kagha to see reason:
Your snake is prepared to kill this child. Do you want her death on your hands?(if passive DC 5 Nature check passed)
[INTIMIDATION]Imprisoning her empowers the refugees. Are you willing to risk insurrection?(DC 10)
[BARBARIAN][STRENGTH]Call off your snake or it goes in my dinner pot tonight.(DC 10)
[BERSERKER][INTIMIDATION]CALL OFF YOUR WORM.(DC 10)
[PERSUASION]Let the child go, so she can spread word to the others of your might.(DC 10)
[TIEFLING][PERSUASION]Let the child go, so she can spread word to the others of your might.(DC 15)
If succeeding any of these checks, Arabella is saved. Otherwise, Teela bites and kills Arabella. Whatever the outcome, the party can report it to her parents, which completes the quest.
If Save the Refugees is resolved in favor of the tieflings, Arabella's fate has significant consequences:
Arabella's survival is a key factor in Kagha's fate
If Arabella survives, she can be found in:
Act Two, starting the quest Find Arabella's Parents
Act Three, as part of Gather Your Allies (provided she survives Act Two)
Passing a Persuasion or Nature check:
+8 +5 -1 +5 +5
Passing the Intimidation check as Lae'zel or talking to Teela with  Speak with Animals as a Druid:
+8
In either case, if Gale has been recruited, his approval increases even if he is not present. This is one of several actions which advance the first stage of his personal quest, The Wizard of Waterdeep.
45 XP
 Komira's Locket, if Arabella is saved
Not On My Watch ( Urchin background)
Snake Whisperer ( Folk Hero background)

Search for Halsin in the Goblin Camp: The druid Halsin was last seen on an expedition into goblin territory.
The druid Rath told us that the First Druid Halsin might be the only one capable of stopping Kagha from finishing the ritual.
The adventurers were with Halsin when he disappeared. They might know what happened to him.
We learned that Halsin turned into a bear during the chaos of the failed expedition. He could still be disguised in that shape.
We learned that the goblins imprisoned the survivors deep within their camp. Halsin could be held captive there - if he survived.
We found a group of goblins massacred by some kind of animal. Could this have been the work of the missing druid Halsin?
We saved a large bear who turned out to be a druid named Halsin. He asked us to kill the goblin leaders so he could restore peace to the grove.
We killed Dror Ragzlin, the goblin king.
The drow commander Minthara is dead.
We ended Priestess Gut's rituals - and her life.
We defeated all three goblin leaders. We should report to Halsin.
The goblins left to attack the grove. We should report to Halsin.
Defend the Emerald Grove from the goblin: The goblins have begun their raid on the grove. Halsin has gone there to fend them off - and hopefully mend the rift between the druids and tieflings.
We saved a large bear, and it ended up being the druid Halsin himself. He asked for help defeating the goblins before they destroy the grove.
We helped Halsin save the grove. Hopefully, he can resolve the conflict between the tieflings and the druids. We'll find out when we meet him there.
Halsin has returned to the grove. We should speak to him.
A large bear we saved turned out to be a druid named Halsin. He told us to meet him back at the grove.
The large bear we saved was actually the missing druid Halsin. He told us to meet him back at the grove.
Halsin thanked us and said that Rath would surely reward us for our efforts.
Quest Complete: Halsin was defeated.
Rath was grateful and pointed us to a hidden vault locked by runes in the Enclave Library.
The tieflings left for Baldur's Gate - the grove will be at peace now. Rath should have something for us.
The tieflings left for Baldur's Gate - the grove will be at peace now.
The grove was sealed before we could find Halsin. He won't be needed at the grove anymore.
The tieflings and the druids were defeated. The grove has been destroyed.
Rath thanked us for saving the grove.
Rath is dead.
Halsin thanked us for saving him.
The surviving tieflings have left, and we haven't found Halsin. The grove would likely still benefit from his return.

Save the First Druid.Walkthrough:
This section is an excerpt from Rescue the Druid Halsin § Walkthrough.[edit]
Information on Halsin can be found by speaking to Nettie during Get Help from Healer Nettie or by speaking to Rath. Both Rath and Nettie are in the Inner Sanctum, which is accessible via the stone door north of the Sacred Pool. Mentioning to Rath that Kagha seems dangerous makes him lament that a snake has replaced their leader. Pressing for more information results in Rath revealing that Halsin disappeared at the goblin camp. Nettie discusses more about Halsin if the party is honest with her about their affliction and symptoms, stating that Halsin could better treat them if he was around.
Halsin can be found in the Worg Pens area at X:393Y:-60 of the Shattered Sanctum, in the interior area of the Goblin Camp. While in his wildshape bear form, his goblin captors are harassing him, unaware of his identity. Details on how to approach the Goblin Camp and free Halsin are described below.
This quest can also be completed successfully by initiating the quest Raid the Grove. To accomplish this, the party must reveal the location of the Emerald Grove to  Minthara, causing her to lead an assault on the grove. While besieging the grove, the party can betray her. This strategy lures a good portion of the goblin forces from the Goblin Camp to the grove, causing Halsin to free himself and return to the Grove, once the siege defence is successful.

Entering the Goblin Camp
There are various check-point encounters on the path to the Goblin Camp. There are several methods to approach these, including but not limited to:

A frontal assault.
This approach may lead to turning the entire camp hostile if an alarm drum is successfully sounded.
A mixture of diplomacy checks, such as Persuasion and Deception.
Alternatively, it is possible to brute force through all diplomacy checks by utilizing the Tadpole Power during dialogue.
Drow, or characters disguised as one via the  Disguise Self spell, have special dialogue options to bypass diplomacy checks.
Avoiding the check-points through Stealth or finding an alternative path (i.e. approaching the camp from the north-west).
Approaching from the north-western path requires the party to deal with some sleeping guards who can be woken accidentally if a jumping party member lands near them. A  Silence spell can effectively prevent this.
Upon successfully clearing or bypassing the check-points and entering the camp proper, it is possible to freely partake in Goblin Camp activities, converse with the partying goblins, and take on various side quests (more information on the Goblin Camp page). Specifically for this quest, the party must venture into the interior of the temple.

The Shattered Sanctum
The cracked wall on the second-level of the Goblin Camp.
There are three entrances into the Shattered Sanctum - the Heavy Oak Doors at X:105Y:454, the Passageway at X:51Y:476, or through the Cracked Wall on the upper level of the camp at X:-106Y:481.
The Heavy Oak Doors:

This entrance is guarded by  Warrior Trinzas, who can be persuaded through diplomacy, Tadpole Powers, or drow specific dialogue like the previous check-points.
Violence is always a viable alternative, however, there is a risk the fighting draws the attention of other goblins in the area, leading to the alarms being sounded and all the goblins turning hostile.
The Passageway:

The path to this entrance is "guarded" by three Blast Mines (two charged with Poison Cloud and one with Explosion). Entering by this way places the party at the southeast corner of the Shattered Sanctum.
The Cracked Wall:

The wall is an object with 22 Hit Points, Sturdy, and immunity to all damage types except piercing (resistant) and force (vulnerable).
This makes force-damage items/spells/abilities that can clear the 10 damage threshold - like  Smokepowder Bombs or  Eldritch Blast - ideal for breaking this wall.
There are sleeping guards situation close to the wall. If using the  Silence spell on the explosion or the guards to cover the noise, a confrontation can be avoided.
There is technically a fourth entrance to the temple from the Selûnite Outpost in the Underdark, but that door is inaccessible externally, and must be be opened from inside of the Shattered Sanctum first via the Stone Discs or the wall Lever.

Freeing Halsin
Ornate doors in the Shattered Sanctum leading to the Worg Pens.
Once inside, the party must approach the ornate door at the eastern side of the Shattered Sanctum at X:342Y:32 to enter the Worg Pens, where Halsin is held prisoner. As long as the alarm was not raised, no one stops the party, assuming they are either a True Soul or a welcomed visitor.
Alternatively, a party member can also get taken to the Worg Pens by getting themselves captured - either by offending the goblins (i.e. getting caught pick-pocketing), or through the potential events of the Ask the Goblin Priestess for Help quest (drinking Gut's potion of sleep while not being resistant to it). In this case, the party member is taken to the prison at the Worg Pens, and has the option to break out and find Halsin that way.
Regardless, once in the Worg Pen, approaching the cages triggers a cutscene where the goblins harass Halsin, who is disguised in his bear wildshape. Using  Speak with Animals allows the interacting party member to understand Halsin in his animal form. There are various dialogue options to react to the scenario. Regardless of what happens, in order to free Halsin, the goblins guarding the area must be fought, including  Butcher Vrak and  Beastmaster Zurk.

The Worg Pens where Halsin is kept captive.
At the start of the fight, the goblin youths that were harassing Halsin try to flee and call for assistance. If successful, they draw in the guards outside the doors -  Sharp-eye Neem,  Sharp-eye Eef, and  Sharp-eye Nass.
Once freed, Halsin explains he came here with a group of adventurers to learn more about the mind flayer tadpoles, but was captured. He believes the goblin leaders are planning to assault the grove and offers to join the party as a temporary companion to escape. Accepting his offer turns the entire camp hostile. If Halsin's offer is rejected, he remains in the Worg Pens until the leaders are taken care of.

Priestess Gut
 Priestess Gut can be found in front of the giant throne immediately north of the front entrance at X:297Y:-18. She offers to brand the party with the Brand of the Absolute. Regardless of whether the brand is accepted, Gut recognises the party as fellow True Souls. She attempts to probe into the party's minds with her illithid powers. Allowing her to do this causes her to notice something is amiss. Explaining that it is the result of the tadpoles and asking if she can help, commences Ask the Goblin Priestess for Help. Gut says that the party should join her in her private chambers.
Following her to her chambers, she says that she has a cure for the tadpole and a special cleansing potion must be taken first. However, an Arcana check reveals that it is just a sleeping potion. If called out on this, or if the party member in question is resistant to sleeping potions (i.e. a drow, elf or half-elf), she becomes hostile, triggering several nearby goblins to do the same. However, if the potion is taken, she chains up the party member and says that she intends for them to become her pet squid. It is possible to break free with an Acrobatics or Strength check (Rogues do not need a check to break free). If nothing is done at all, a special cutscene is triggered where  Korrilla appears from a portal and assassinates both Gut and her Ogre guard. She then disappears after a brief conversation, warning the party to be more careful.

Minthara
The drow  Minthara is in the northeastern corner of the Shattered Sanctum at X:333Y:42. There is a  Scrying Eye which must be dealt with before defeating her; otherwise, the entire camp is alerted. Minthara can easily be taken out after speaking with her, as she runs across the bridge to gather the troops. While she is running on the bridge, attack the bridge's posts and she falls into the chasm below. Otherwise, once the scrying eye is dealt with, only Minthara and her minions in the room become hostile, so the other two leaders can still be dealt with stealthily. If Minthara is only  Knocked Out instead of killed, she later can be recruited to the party in Act Two.

Dror Ragzlin
 Dror Ragzlin is unique because he is the only member of the trio who cannot be killed without alerting the two other leaders and the rest of the goblins; killing him, no matter how, turns the entire Shattered Sanctum and Goblin Camp hostile. For this reason, it is best to wrap up all business in the Goblin Camp and acquire the waypoint in the Selûnite Outpost in the [Underdark]] below the Shattered Sanctum before killing or knocking him out.
Dror Ragzlin is in the throne room to the north, casting  Speak with Dead on an illithid corpse at X:296Y:40. A cutscene automatically plays as the party enter the room, with Ragzlin asking if they would like to speak to a squid.
If Ragzlin communes with the illithid without party interference, its killer is revealed to be the party after the second question. This can be avoided by taking control of Ragzlin's questions with a  DC10 Wisdom Check and asking questions other than who is the killer such as "Why was the Nautiloid in Faerûn?" Asking an obvious question like "Who is the Absolute?" causes Ragzlin to be suspicious of the party. Otherwise, if the party is exposed as the illithid's killer, they can talk Ragzlin down through a Persuasion check or by using  Detect Thoughts to reveal that he likes being called the Absolute's Right Hand.

Back in the Worg Pens
Halsin is grateful if the party return and report on the leaders' defeat, and is then willing to answer many questions about his knowledge of tadpoles, as well as the strange movements of Absolutists at Moonrise Towers. He tells the party to return to the Emerald Grove where he will meet them again. Upon returning to the Sacred Pool area, Halsin confronts  Kagha about her actions. He either demotes her status to an initiate or casts her out entirely, depending on whether Investigate Kagha was completed.

Getting the Rewards
If speaking to Rath in the Inner Sanctum at X:463Y:-30, he rewards the party for their service. In the Enclave Library there is a giant stone wolf statue in the room. There are four plaques surrounding the wolf. The party must combine the rune with the empty socket at X:-463Y:24. After interacting with all four runes simultaneously, a hidden staircase is revealed, which leads to a special treasure room.
This section is an excerpt from Rescue the Druid Halsin § Quest rewards.[edit]
Elixir of Poison Resistance
Potion of Animal Speaking - 3x
Potion of Greater Healing - 2x
Robe of Summer
Scroll of Animal Friendship - 2x
Scroll of Hold Person
Sorrow
Wyvern Toxin
 Halsin joins camp, and can become a potential companion in Act Two.
This section is an excerpt from Rescue the Druid Halsin § Companion approval.[edit]
Release Halsin from the worg pens: +1
This section is an excerpt from Rescue the Druid Halsin § Notes.[edit]

Once Dror Ragzlin is killed, the entire goblin camp, inside and out, becomes hostile. If the party spared Sazza from Minthara, Sazza may voice disapproval but does not become hostile. Thus, the She Cannot Be Caged! achievement can still be achieved.
Traveling to Rosymorn Monastery Trail and back causes Halsin to die, concluding this quest. This happens even if the party avoid any long rests during the transition, either forced or voluntary.
If having travelled to the Rosymorn Monastery Trail before doing a long rest, the celebration does not trigger. Then  Halsin can be found waiting at the Sacred Pool, where the party can talk to him; this works after talking to  Art Cullagh in the Last Light Inn in Act Two. The "Celebrate at camp" objective is skipped in this case.[verify]

The goblin Sazza is a prisoner in the grove. She's promised us an introduction to her tribe - and their powerful healer - if we break her out.
We broke Sazza out. Now for the hard part - getting her out of the grove.
We got out of the grove in one piece. Sazza told us to meet her at the Goblin Camp.
Thanks to Sazza, we made it to the camp. She told us to meet her further inside, where she'll introduce us to the drow True Soul.
We found Sazza talking to the drow. She still owes us information - maybe this drow has it?
Quest Complete: Sazza died.
Sazza died while trying to escape the grove.
We didn't free Sazza. Who knows what happened to her?
We rescued Sazza but didn't get the information she promised.
We never met Sazza at the Goblin Camp.

Once again, we saved Sazza. She owes us - big time.
Sazza betrayed us, but we convinced Minthara to spare her life. Let's hope she'll remember what we did for her.

Save the Goblin Sazza.Walkthrough:
When arriving in the Emerald Grove for the first time,  Sazza is caged in the Makeshift Prison at X:189Y:587. If the party entered the Grove through the main gate,  Arka is in the prison threatening to shoot Sazza to avenge her brother,  Kanon. Meanwhile,  Memnos tries to calm her, insisting that Sazza is not the goblin that killed her brother. The party has several options on how to proceed:
Shut up. Those words could be your last.
Step between the crossbow and the goblin.Random +1 to +8
step out of the way. +1
This- This is wrong, isn't it?
[PALADIN]This goblin is in need of judgement. I should decide her fate.
[DARK URGE]Stand back, and admire the guard's overflowing hate.
Do nothing.
If the party choose to stand between Arka and Sazza, Arka tells them to get out of the way:
[INTIMIDATION]Lower the crossbow, or I'll deliver you right to your brother.(DC 10)
[PERSUASION]No, shoot me. Should be easy, right?(DC 10)
[PERSUASION]This won't avenge Kanon. It won't change anything.(DC 10)
[DETECT THOUGHTS][WISDOM]Read the tiefling's thoughts.
What would Kanon think of you now?
[BERSERKER][BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]ROAR.(DC 10)
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]Take your shot. Go on.(DC 10)
[OATH OF THE CROWN][INTIMIDATION]If you take the law into your own hands, I'll have to stop you.(DC 10)
[GOOD CLERIC][PERSUASION]Killing this goblin won't help you. Let the gods provide comfort.(DC 10)
[MONK][INSIGHT]I sense the pain you carry - but killing this creature will not ease your burden(DC 10)
Step out of the way.
If the  Intimidation Check or one of the  Persuasion Check rolls fail, the player character can make another roll that they previously did not choose.
If the party cannot persuade Arka to relent, she shoots Sazza and the quest is not received. Otherwise, she storms off and Sazza calls out to the party.
Alternatively, the party can head west of the grove's main gate following the initial goblin attack instead of immediately entering the grove, until they come across a hidden stone door. A successful perception check highlights the door itself, while a survival check reveals goblin tracks leading up to it. This route is considerably more dangerous than entering the grove normally, as it is guarded by statues that continuously shoot fire bolts at anyone within range, flammable explosives, and a small party of goblins holding the druid  Findal captive. The statues are activated by runes embedded in the walls, which can be turned off by directly interacting with them or using  Mage Hand. On the opposite side of the tunnel, an exit leads directly into the Makeshift Prison. This route allows the party to approach Sazza before Arka does, skipping the dialogue with her.
If Sazza survives through either approach, she attempts to convince the party to help her escape. She explains that her goddess, the Absolute, protects her and that the goblins will wreck the entire Grove. The party will be spared, if they aid her in returning to camp. The party can free Sazza or leave her to her fate. To free Sazza, the cage must be lockpicked with  DC10 Sleight of Hand Check, smashed by force, or unlocked with  Iron Cell Key. Freeing Sazza rewards 15 XP.
Sazza can be questioned, but she refuses to answer or she'll be a rat's breakfast:
[BERSERKER][INTIMIDATION]TELL ME, OR YOU'LL BE MY BREAKFAST.(DC 5)
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]Tell me, or you'll be my breakfast.(DC 5)
[INTIMIDATION]Bad news for me. Good news for the spiders.(DC 5)
[PERSUASION]I've saved your hide twice now. You owe me.(DC 5)
[DETECT THOUGHTS][WISDOM]Read her mind.
You're in trouble no matter what. You may as well talk.
Once free, Sazza must be escorted from the Grove and becomes an Attached Follower. Her inventory cannot be accessed, but Sazza can be controlled and can interact with melee weapons but not ranged weapons on the ground in order to equip them. She can be used in combat, or be told to stay still.
The party can stealthily escape the Grove by jumping on the rocks behind Sazza's cage and opening the stone door to the Underground Passage behind the prison. If the party did not previously access the tunnels, they must deactivate the statues and deal with the goblins in the passage.
Warrior Gresh recognises Sazza and faults her for the failed Grove raid. He and his group become hostile and must be defeated to proceed. To exit the Underground Passage, the party can follow the path to the stone door at the other end at X:-422Y:-236.
It is also possible to escort Sazza through the front gate by passing several  Deception Check or  Persuasion Check checks, or simply slaughtering the tieflings.
If Sazza is successfully escorted from the Grove, she departs and agrees to meet the party at the Goblin Camp. She can later be found at the wooden bridge near the camp at X:-60Y:364, where she convinces the guards to allow the party entrance. The party must continue to the Shattered Sanctum where Sazza will again negotiate a path for the party. If these guards have been killed before Sazza can talk to them, then Sazza runs away and may not show up during the next step, in which case the quest cannot proceed. However, after a long rest, she may show up next to Minthara in the Shattered Sanctum.
Inside the Shattered Sanctum, Sazza speaks with  Minthara at X:331Y:43, and explains that the raiders they have been searching for are at the Grove. Minthara is angry that Sazza did not slaughter the raiders and the Grove, and threatens to feed her to the spiders. In an attempt to survive, Sazza turns on the party and presents them as prisoners. However, Minthara recognises them as True Souls and is more determined to feed Sazza to the spiders. The party can vouch for Sazza, call her an idiot, or stand by as Sazza is killed. If Sazza is getting fed to the spiders, then the party can intervene once more to save her.
Assassin's Touch for escorting Sazza to the Goblin Camp
If Sazza was successfully escorted out of the grove, the inspiration Jailbreaker is granted to party members with the  Criminal background.
Dealing with captive Sazza in the Emerald Grove prison:
Allow Arka to shoot Sazza: +1 +1
Stand between Arka's crossbow and Sazza: +6
Successfully stop Arka from shooting Sazza (successful intimidation or persuasion DC roll): +3 +1 -1 +1 -1
Talking to Sazza while she is in the cage: "Deal. I'll get you out of there." +1 -1 -1
Talking to Sazza after freeing her from her cage:
"It's a deal, signed and struck": -1 -1 -1
In the Goblin camp:
Defend Sazza before Minthara: -1 +1 +1 -1 -1
Do not defend Sazza before Minthara "I explained everything to her.": +1 -1 +1 +1
She Cannot Be Caged!
Rescue Sazza from the Emerald Grove, Goblin Camp and Moonrise Towers in one playthrough.
As of Hotfix #35 - 14:25, 20 November 2025 (CET), if the party agree with  Minthara to throw  Sazza in the spider pit and allow Minthara to raid the Emerald Grove, nothing actually happens. Minthara and the goblins leave the room and Sazza remains in place. She cannot be spoken to. The quest is stuck on the objective Speak to Sazza and the drow. Even after killing all the goblins in the Goblin Camp and the raiders in the Emerald Grove, Sazza remains in the room and the quest never updates. If this happens, Sazza can be killed and the quest completed, but it does not grant the 45 XP awarded for saving her or throwing her to the spiders.
If killing or knocking out  Minthara before fulfilling the Speak to Sazza and the drow quest objective, Sazza cannot be spoken to anymore; she also sometimes runs away and disappears from the game instead. In either case, she does not appear at Moonrise Towers in Act Two.
Standing between Arka's crossbow and Sazza - is giving gale random amounts did it 4 time and got 4 different amounts  +0+3+6 +8[verify]

Save the Refugees.Walkthrough:
While exploring the Emerald Grove, the party encounter a group of tiefling refugees taking shelter in the grove. The tieflings are attempting to reach Baldur's Gate after their exile from Elturel. However, the area is swarming with goblin raiders scouting for and launching attacks on the grove, which has led the local druids to begin the Rite of Thorns to close the grove off from the outside world, which would leave the tieflings vulnerable.
The ritual can be stopped by either killing the goblin leaders in the quest Defeat the Goblins, or dealing with  Kagha and the Shadow Druids in the quest Investigate Kagha. However, in order to ensure safe passage for the tieflings, the goblins must be defeated.
After killing the goblin leaders, the tieflings are ready to leave for Baldur's Gate. Before departing, they join the party at their camp for a celebration. This grants the Guardian of the Forgotten to party members with the Folk Hero background.
This quest automatically transitions when travelling to Rosymorn Monastery Trail or taking the elevator out of the Grymforge. A message pops up indicating to finish business before proceeding. If continuing, the quest transitions with the refugees being killed and the grove sealed (as if one sided/let the Shadow druids seal the grove). Despite the grove being sealed, it is still possible to enter: starting from the location of where one finds  Edowin, it is possible to  Fly up north to a path through the mountains. From there, the party can go farther north and reach a bramble wall sealing off the grove, but it is possible to fly past the bramble wall, thus entering the sealed off grove. Inside the sealed off grove, all the druids are still at their regular locations, but all are now hostile and attack on sight.
While it was believed that the quest would transition after a certain number of long rests, this has been proven false.[1]
Conversation with Zevlor
Agree to help the refugees. -1 +1 +3 -1 +5
Decline to help the refugees. +2 -1 +1 +1 -1
Defeat the Goblins
Investigate Kagha
Kill Kagha
Raid the Emerald Grove
Save the First Druid
↑  Proxy Gate Tactician (2023-09-08). Busting More Myths in Baldur's Gate 3. YouTube.

Steal the Sacred Idol.Walkthrough:
In order to receive this quest, the party must be on good terms with the tiefling children in the Grove. A variety of quests influence how Mol sees them:
Find Your Belongings - Not threatening the tiefling children
Investigate the Beach - Saving  Mirkon from the Harpies
Return the Locket - Resolving the conflict with  Meli keeping the locket
Save Arabella - Saving  Arabella from Teela
The most effective way to gain Mol's respect is to rescue Mirkon. He informs the party about the location of the tiefling hideout and the password needed to enter, allowing a simple path forward. Once inside, Mol greets the party and thanks them for all the good deeds they've done for the children. Choosing Thought you might need help with something results in Mol requesting the party steal the  Idol of Silvanus as revenge for hurting Arabella and the other tiefling refugees. Agreeing to steal the idol starts the quest. If not choosing the dialogue option to help Mol the party is locked out of the quest and its reward.
The Idol of Silvanus is in the center of the Sacred Pool X:243Y:507 right in the middle of the Rite of Thorns ritual and surrounded by druids. Stealing the idol while the ritual is underway starts a cutscene where a signal is given and the druids commence a pre-arranged slaughter of every tiefling within the Grove. The druids also become hostile to the party with the notable exception of  Rath who always sides with the tieflings as well as  Findal if he was found and healed in the Underground Passage. Since the fight happens in real time, leaving the tieflings to their fate (or not moving quickly enough to defend as many of them as possible) causes them to be killed by the Emerald Grove druids. The final stand takes place in the makeshift prison where all the tiefling civilians are holed up. If enough of them are killed then Save the Refugees fails.  Zevlor is usually one of the first to fall since he faces  Kagha in battle.
If the party is caught, fighting the druids may still be avoided with a  DC15 Deception or Intimidation Check.
The idol can be stolen without angering the druids if Investigate Kagha has been completed and the Rite of Thorns has been stopped. However, this quest has to be started before stopping the ritual or it will not be offered. After this, the party may use stealth to discreetly acquire the idol. Spells like  Darkness,  Fog Cloud, and  Greater Invisibility are also helpful.
Another option to avoid getting caught is to hide, steal the idol, and fast travel before being spoken to by a druid. If returning to the grove with enough delay, the druids do not become hostile. Returning to Mol still triggers the normal quest dialog.
If the druids in the ritual circle are killed without being seen (using spells like  Darkness,  Invisibility, or  Fog Cloud), the party can take the idol. The quest says "now the druids are attacking the tieflings" but the massacre cutscene does not trigger and no such attack occurs.
Ring of Protection
Heretic ( Criminal backgrounds)
Idolatry ( Urchin backgrounds)
After completing the quest, the idol can be bought back (or pickpocketed) from Mol, allowing the idol's bonuses to be kept. The price depends on the character's Charisma and standing with her.
It is not possible to start this quest after finishing Investigate Kagha, Save the First Druid, or anything that decides the grove's fate prior to speaking with Mol.
It is, however, possible to start this quest before finishing any of those quests, but steal and turn in the idol after the grove's fate is decided (assuming Mol is still alive and present) ([imgur Image by InnerCrayon - 2025-07-04).

Evil is inscribed on every page of this book. But all evil has a weakness - there must be a way to destroy it.
Only light from the divine can burn away this book's evil.
Quest Complete: The book has been destroyed, along with any dark secrets it contained. Good riddance.
The book was too valuable to be destroyed - we decided to take its knowledge for ourselves.
The book was lost, along with any dark secrets it contained.

Destroy the Ancient Tome.Walkthrough:
The Necromancy of Thay is obtained during Search the Cellar in the apothecary's secret laboratory, accessible past the  Ornate Mirror. Removing the book from its trapped room initiates a quest to either unlock the Necromancy of Thay's secrets or destroy it forever.
The book can only be destroyed by dropping it on the ground and attacking it with Radiant damage, like  Guiding Bolt. Destroying nets disapproval from  Astarion, but approval from  Karlach,  Lae'zel, and  Wyll. Destroying the book also spawns 3 hostile Shadows.
Throwing the book into a chasm causes it to bounce back to the thrower.

We found the journal of a blacksmith's apprentice. It mentioned blueprints to create masterwork weapons. They must be nearby.
We found blueprints for masterwork weapons. All we need to forge one is some bark from a Sussur Tree.
We found the blueprints the journal mentioned. If we find some bark from a sussur tree, we could forge a masterwork weapon.
We found a Sussur Tree. Now we need to take some of its bark.
We've got the Sussur Bark - now we need to head back to the forge.
Quest Completed!: We forged a masterwork dagger/sickle/greatsword.

Finish the Masterwork Weapon.Walkthrough:
After starting the quest, the  Sussur Tree Bark can be obtained from the Sussur Tree in the Dread Hollow in the Underdark X:-14Y:-138.
To forge a weapon, use the blacksmith's bellows to light the furnace in the Whispering Depths / Blighted Village at X:-446Y:-378. Once lit, Sussur Tree Bark must be combined with the furnace, which turns the flames from red to blue. Finally, using a plain dagger, greatsword, or sickle in the active furnace results in creating a Sussur weapon with +1 enchantment and the ability to silence on hit.
Forging a Sussur weapon grants the inspiration The Finest Ingredients to party members with the Guild Artisan background.
One of:
Sussur Dagger
Sussur Greatsword
Sussur Sickle

Rescue the Gnome.Walkthrough:
The only objective of this quest is to free  Barcus Wroot. Approaching the windmill at X:13Y:418 automatically initiates a conversation with the goblin leader  Fezzerk. There are several options to resolve the situation.
There are a handful of ways of solving the encounter peacefully without combat:
Intimidate, deceive, or persuade
[BRANDED][DECEPTION]We're allies - I have the Absolute's Brand. Leave the gnome to me.(DC 5)
[DECEPTION]We're allies, you and I. Servants of the Absolute.(DC 15)
[LAE'ZEL][INTIMIDATION]You dare refuse a githyanki warrior?(DC 15)
[INTIMIDATION]I won't let you harm a defenseless being - not without a fight.(DC 15)
[WISDOM]Read the goblin's mind.
You don't have the stomach for a fight.
[INTIMIDATION]Negotiations are over. Either leave or die.(DC 15)
[BERSERKER][INTIMIDATION]ROAR.(DC 15)
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]Mine.(DC 15)
[PALADIN][INTIMIDATION]I'm not bargaining with you. Free him, or I swear on my oath, you will die.(DC 15)
[PERSUASION]All of you lot against one deep gnome? You should go find a better target.(DC 15)
[PERSUASION]You've had your fun - it's high time you found someone else to torment.(DC 15)
[HALF-ORC][INTIMIDATION]Don't utter a word. Just stare down the goblin.(DC 10)
[PALADIN][INTIMIDATION]I consider the gnome under my protection. You don't want to fight me for him.(DC 15)
[BRANDED][DECEPTION]Show him your brand and say you're taking the gnome. The Absolute wills it.(DC 5)
[BARBARIAN]Your skull will make a fine chalice one I plug those beady eye sockets.
[FIGHTER]You're making a mistake. You can think it over in the Fugue Plane.
Using the tadpole
[TRUE SOUL][ILLITHID][WISDOM]You will release the gnome. That's an order.(DC 2)
[TRUE SOUL][ILLITHID][WISDOM]I am a True Soul. Now once again - release the gnome.(DC 2)
If convincing the goblins to walk away, the party is rewarded experience as if they defeated the whole group.
Demand you be allowed to pass
If trying to pass, Fezzerk claims that this is also what Barcus wanted, and demands 1000 gold, leading to several options:
Pay the goblin.
[PERSUASION]I might be willing to part with a more reasonable sum. (100)(DC 15)
[PERSUASION]Here's my counter-offer: a far more reasonable sum. (500)(DC 10)
[PERSUASION]That's a ridiculous amount. I'll make you a realistic offer. (250)(DC 10)
[PERSUASION]Haggle with the goblin, though you lack even a modest sum of gold.(DC 15)
[WISDOM]Read the goblin's mind.
Your price is absurd and you know it.
[DECEPTION]I can pay twice that much. I just have to go and fetch it.(DC 15)
[PERSUASION]Here's my counter-offer: you can all live so long as you leave right now.(DC 15)
[MONK]This will lead only to pain, not fulfillment.
[ROGUE]Provide directions to a non-existant cache of gold.
[BERSERKER][INTIMIDATION]OR YOU CAN LEAVE. ALIVE.(DC 15)
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]Leave. Or die.(DC 15)
No matter which of the above checks succeed, Fezzerk lowers the price to 5 because he cannot count. Passing the Illithid Persuasion check can force his group to leave without payment. If paid, the party members are allowed to leave, but if approaching again, a fight breaks out.
[BARD]Not an orator, eh? I could teach you how to talk gold out of any purse, for free passage.
[PERFORMANCE]Wait! Just let me go...please.(DC 5)
[ILLITHID][WISDOM]I'm going to pay nothing and you and your cronies are going to leave.(DC 5)
This causes Fezzerk and his group to leave, rewarding experience as if the party defeated them all.
Approach as a drow
Drow characters (or those disguised as one) can demand an explanation or demand that Fezzerk leaves. This causes Fezzerk to be nervous, and he asks the talking party member if they are a True Soul:
Of course I'm a True Soul. How dare you even question the fact?(if aware of true souls)
[PERFORMANCE]I don't answer to the likes of you.(DC 5)
[DECEPTION]Naturally - do I look cursed to you?(DC 5)
[INTIMIDATION]Say nothing. Stare down the gibbering coward.(DC 5)
[BERSERKER][INTIMIDATION]ROAR.(DC 5)
[BARBARIAN][INTIMIDATION]Did I say you could ask questions?(DC 5)
[OATH OF THE ANCIENTS][INTIMIDATION]I am nature's vanguard, you are but a stench on the breeze. Begone.(DC 5)
[OATH OF DEVOTION][INTIMIDATION]I am duty bound to Virtue itself. You are in my way. Leave this place.(DC 5)
[OATH OF VENGEANCE][INTIMIDATION]I am the fear-dream of disloyal whelps. You are starting to irk me. Leave.(DC 5)
[OATH OF THE CROWN][INTIMIDATION]I represent the law, which all must obey. And I order you to leave.(DC 5)
[ILLITHID]I am a True Soul. Your prisoner is now mine.(DC 5)
[ILLITHID]I am a True Soul. Your prisoner is now mine.(if aware of true souls)
[BRANDED]Show them your brand.
[BRANDED]Show them your brand and tell them to carry on.
All other options start combat.
If attacking the goblins without attempting to convince them to stop or let the party pass (it is possible to ask what and why he is doing this first) nets +1 -1 +5.
If a fight breaks out, he surrenders when nearly killed, causing the rest of his group to flee. It is possible to interrogate him, demand his possessions, or kill him. Experience is not granted for the goblins that run away, so to obtain the most experience while engaging in Fezzerk's surrender requires the party to kill all other goblins first.
Barbarians have a unique dialogue path:
[WISDOM]Ignore the rage. Listen to your head. There is nothing to gain from being rash.(DC 10)
[BERSERKER]Give in. KILL THE MAGGOT.
Leave reason to the reasonable. Kill the runt.
Otherwise, the party can interrogate him:
I want the gold you took. Now.
Give me my gold back - and anything else you have, too.
Talk and you might live. If it's worth my while.
Your valuables - now.
I want everything of value you have.
Your valuables are mine now. Hand them over.
... or just kill or release him:
No mercy. Kill the goblin. +1 -1 +1 +1 +5
Run along, goblin. Before I change my mind. -1 -1 -5
Once the goblins are dealt with, the party must enter the interior of the windmill to pull the brake, allowing them to free Barcus. There are two levers inside the windmill. The "Brake" lever must be chosen to safely stop the windmill. The "Release Brake" lever causes the windmill machinery to spin faster, which launches Barcus into the sky by inertia, subsequently killing him. His body can be found on the western road leading out of the Blighted Village.
If the windmill is stopped properly, the party can free Barcus by interacting with him. Barcus offers the party his luggage in the windmill basement, which contains various goods, including a  Smokepowder Satchel. He reasons that lugging the extra weight resulted in the goblins capturing him. Saving Barcus is not required to obtain his possessions, as his luggage is in the basement regardless.
Smokepowder Bomb carried by Fezzerk
Smokepowder Satchel
The Speedy Lightfeet in the heavy chest next to Barcus' heavy backpack
Very Heavy Greataxe carried by Fezzerk
45 XP for saving Barcus Wroot
190 XP for making Fezzerk and his group to leave.
210 XP for defeating all of Fezzerk's group. After one combat round or so, the rest of the group flees slowly. Besides killing the two devouts and Fezzerk, they do not grant any additional XP.
280 XP for making Fezzerk and his group to leave, going turn based mode to attack them, defeating the two goblin devouts and Fezzerk last.
Saving Barcus has far reaching consequences for many quests, including Save the Grymforge Gnomes, Rescue Wulbren, and Avenge the Ironhands. If rescued, he can be encountered again throughout Acts Two and Three.
Exploring the Blighted Village or the area north of its walls may trigger the appearance of  Barcus and the goblin group without triggering the quest.
Entering Grymforge without rescuing Barcus (even without initiating the quest) causes the goblins to disappear and Barcus to die, locking the party out of this quest and future quests involving Barcus.

"We found a journal that mentioned a cellar - along with some powerful items. This sounds promising and, more importantly, valuable."
"We found an apothecary's ledger. It mentions some rare plants stored in a cellar - could be worth finding."
"We found the cellar mentioned by the journal. Time to have a look around."
"The owner of the cellar was more than a doctor - he was a necromancer. And he was obsessed with a particular book."
Decide what to do with the book of necro: "We found a strange and powerful tome, 'The Necromancy of Thay'. What should we do with it?"
"Books like these contain manifold secrets - though they've been known to drive people mad."
Quest Complete: "We deciphered the final page of 'The Necromancy of Thay', and finished the book."
"The book was destroyed, along with any secrets it had."
"The book was lost, along with its secrets."
"The 'Tharchiate Codex', key to finishing 'The Necromancy of Thay', was destroyed."
